verses: śrī⋅yamunā⋅snāna⋅vandanā kalinda⋅tanaye! devi! paramānanda⋅vardhini! snāmi te salile sarvāparādhānmāṁ vimocaya śrī⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅snāna⋅vandanā rādhikā⋅sama⋅saubhāgyaṁ sarvatīrtha⋅pravanditaṁ prasīda rādhikā⋅kuṇḍa! snāmi te salile śubhe śrī⋅śyāma⋅kuṇḍa⋅snāna⋅vandanā udbhutaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅pādābjā⋅dariṣṭa⋅vadhataś⋅chalāt pāhi māṁ pāmaraṁ snāmi śyāma⋅kuṇḍa! jale tava śrī⋅paurṇamāsī⋅vandanā rādheśa⋅keli⋅prabhutā⋅vinoda⋅ vinyāśa⋅vijñāṁ vraja⋅vanditāṅghrim kṛpālutādyakhila⋅viśvandyāṁ śrī⋅paurṇamāsīṁ śirasā namāmi śrī⋅vṛndā⋅devī⋅vandanā vṛndāvana⋅sthira⋅carān paripālayitri! vṛnde! tayorasikayoritisaubhagena āḍhyāsi tat kuru kṛpāṁ gaṇanā jathaiva śrī⋅rādhikā⋅parijaneṣu mamāpi siddhyet śrī⋅saṅkalpa⋅kalpa⋅drumaḥ bhaktyā vihīnā aparādha⋅lakṣaiḥ kṣiptās ca kāmādi⋅taraṅga⋅madhye kṛpāmayi! tvāṁ śaraṇaṁ prapannā vṛnde numaste caraṇāravindam śrī⋅vṛndādevyaṣṭakam śrī⋅vraja⋅vāsi⋅vṛnda⋅vandanā mudā jatra brahmā tṛṇa⋅nikara⋅gulmādiṣu paraṁ sadā kāṅkṣan janmārpita⋅vividha⋅karmāpyanudinam kramād je tatraiva vraja⋅bhuvi vasanti priya⋅janā mayā te te vandyāḥ parama⋅vinayāḥ puṇya⋅khacitāḥ śrī⋅vraja⋅vilāsa⋅stavaḥ śrī⋅gopīśvara⋅śiva⋅vandanā vṛndāvanāvani⋅pate jaya soma soma⋅maule! sanaka⋅sanandana⋅sanātana⋅nāradeḍya gopīśvara vraja⋅vilāsi jugāṅghri⋅padme prema prayaccha nirupādhi namo namaste śrī⋅saṅkalpa⋅kalpa⋅drumaḥ śrī⋅tulasī⋅vandanā mahā⋅prasāda⋅jananī sarva⋅saubhāgya⋅vardhinī ādhi⋅vyādhi⋅harī nityaṁ tulasi! tvaṁ namo’stu te śrī⋅gaṅgā⋅vandanā prabhu⋅krīḍāpātrīmamṛta⋅rasa⋅gātrīm ṛṣi⋅ghaṭā⋅ śiva⋅brahmendrādīḍita⋅mahita⋅māhātmya⋅mukharām lasat⋅kiñjalkāmbhojani⋅madhupa⋅garbhoru⋅karuṇā⋅ mahaṁ vande gaṅgāmagha⋅nikara⋅bhaṅgājala⋅kaṇām śrī⋅vedavyāsa⋅deva⋅vandanā pitā⋅parāśaro jasya śukadevasya jaḥ pitā taṁ vyāsaṁ vadarī⋅vāsaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅dvaipāyanaṁ bhaje śrī⋅siddhānta⋅darpaṇam śrī⋅śukadeva⋅vandanā jaṁ pravajantam⋅anupetam⋅apeta⋅kṛtyaṁ dvaipāyano viraha⋅kātara ājuhāva putreti tan⋅maya⋅tayā taravo’bhinedus⋅ taṁ sarva⋅bhūta⋅hṛdayaṁ munim⋅ānato’smi jaḥ svānubhāvam⋅akhila⋅śruti⋅sāram⋅ekam⋅ adhyātma⋅dīpam⋅atititīrṣatāṁ tamo’ndham saṁsāriṇāṁ karuṇayāha purāṇa⋅guhyaṁ taṁ vyāsa⋅sūnum⋅upayāmi guruṁ munīnām śrī.bhā. 1.2.2-3 śrīmad⋅bhāgavata⋅vandanā tamādi⋅devaṁ karuṇā⋅nidhānaṁ tamāla⋅varṇaṁ su⋅hitāvatāram apāra⋅saṁsāra⋅samudra⋅setuṁ bhajāmahe bhāgavata⋅svarūpam śrī⋅padma⋅purāṇa śrī⋅śrī⋅nāma⋅vandanā jayati jayati nāmānanda⋅rūpaṁ murārer viramita⋅nija⋅dharma⋅dhyāna⋅pūjādi⋅yatnam katham⋅api sakṛd āttaṁ mukti⋅daṁ prāṇināṁ jat paramam⋅amṛtam⋅ekaṁ jīvanaṁ bhūṣaṇaṁ me śrī⋅bṛhad⋅bhāgavatāmṛtama madhura⋅madhuram etan maṅgalāṁ maṅgalānāṁ sakala⋅nigama⋅vallī⋅sat⋅phalaṁ cit⋅svarūpam sakṛd api parigītaṁ śraddhayā helayā vā bhṛgu⋅vara! nara⋅mātraṁ tārayet kṛṣṇa⋅nāma śrī⋅hari⋅bhakti⋅vilāsa śrī⋅śrī⋅jagannātha⋅deva⋅vandanā mahāmbhodhes tīre kanaka⋅rucire nīla⋅śikhare vasan prasādāntaḥ sahaja⋅bala⋅bhadreṇa balinā subhadrā⋅madhyasthaḥ sakala⋅sura⋅sevā⋅vasarado⋅ jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana⋅pathagāmī bhavatu me śrī⋅śrī⋅jagannāthāṣṭakama nīlācala⋅nivāsāya nityāya paramātmane balabhadra⋅subhadrābhyāṁ jagannāthāya te namaḥ śrī⋅śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅deva⋅vandanā namaste narasiŋhāya prahlādāhlāda⋅dāyine hiraṇyakaśipor⋅vakṣaḥ śilā⋅ṭaṅka⋅nakhālaye śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅purāṇa ito nṛsiŋhaḥ parato nṛsiŋho jato jato jāmi tato nṛsiŋhaḥ bahir⋅nṛsiŋho hṛdaye nṛsiŋho nṛsiŋham⋅ādiṁ śaraṇaṁ prapadye śrī⋅nṛsiŋha⋅purāṇa śrī⋅nṛsiŋha, jaya nṛsiŋha, jaya jaya nṛsiŋha prahlādeśa jaya padmā⋅mukha⋅padma⋅bhṛṅga vāgīśā jasya vadane lakṣmīr⋅yasya ca vakṣasi jasyāste hṛdaye saṁvit taṁ nṛsiŋham⋅ahaṁ bhaje prahlāda⋅hṛdayā⋅hlādaṁ bhaktāvidyā⋅vidāraṇam śarad⋅indu⋅ruciṁ vande pārīndra⋅vadanaṁ harim śrī⋅bhāvārtha⋅dīpikā śrī⋅śrī⋅rāmacandra⋅vandanā nīlāmbuja⋅śyāmala⋅komalāṅgaṁ sītā⋅samāropita⋅vāma⋅bhāgam pāṇau mahāsāyaka⋅cārucāpaṁ namāmi rāmaṁ raghu⋅vaṁśa⋅nātham śrī⋅rāma⋅carita⋅mānasa dakṣiṇe lakṣmaṇo dhanvī vāmato jānakī śubhā purato mārutīr jasya taṁ namāmi raghūtamam śrī⋅śrī⋅daśāvatāra⋅vandanā vedān uddharate jaganti vahate bhū⋅golam udvibrate daityān dārayate baliṁ chalayate kṣatra⋅kṣayaṁ kurvate paulastyaṁ jayate halaṁ kalayate kāruṇyam⋅ātanvate mlecchān⋅mūrchayate daśākṛti⋅kṛte kṛṣṇāya tubhyaṁ namaḥ śrī⋅gīta⋅govindama
en_translation: śrī-yamunā-snāna-vandanā O Kalinda-tanayā! O Devī! O increaser of supreme bliss! I bathe in your waters; please liberate me from all offenses. śrī-rādhā-kuṇḍa-snāna-vandanā Possessing good fortune equal to Rādhikā, worshipped by all tīrthas. Be pleased, O Rādhikā-kuṇḍa! I bathe in your auspicious waters. śrī-śyāma-kuṇḍa-snāna-vandanā Manifested from the lotus foot of Kṛṣṇa on the pretext of killing Ariṣṭa. Protect me, a fallen soul; I bathe in your waters, O Śyāma-kuṇḍa! śrī-paurṇamāsī-vandanā Expert in arranging the various playful pastimes of the master of Rādhā, whose lotus feet are worshipped by Vraja. Worshipped by the entire universe for her mercy and other qualities. I bow my head to Śrī Paurṇamāsī. śrī-vṛndā-devī-vandanā O protectress of the moving and non-moving living entities of Vṛndāvana! O Vṛndā! By the good fortune of those two Rasikas, you are wealthy. Please grant mercy so that I may be counted among the attendants of Śrī Rādhikā. Devoid of devotion, tossed by hundreds of thousands of offenses into the waves of lust and other vices. O merciful one! I have taken shelter of you. O Vṛndā, I bow to your lotus feet. śrī-vraja-vāsi-vṛnda-vandanā Where Brahmā joyfully desires birth among the grass and bushes, always daily offering various activities. Those dear people who reside there in the land of Vraja are worshipable by me with supreme humility; they are inlaid with piety. śrī-gopīśvara-śiva-vandanā O master of the land of Vṛndāvana, glories to you, O Soma, O Soma-maule! Worshipped by Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanātana, and Nārada. O Gopīśvara, O enjoyer of Vraja, please bestow unconditional prema at your two lotus feet. Obeisances, obeisances unto you. śrī-tulasī-vandanā Mother of Mahā-prasāda, increaser of all good fortune. Remover of mental and physical diseases eternally, O Tulasī! Obeisances unto you. śrī-gaṅgā-vandanā The vessel of the Lord's pastimes, whose body is nectarean rasa, whose greatness is loudly proclaimed by the assembly of sages, Śiva, Brahmā, and Indra. Filled with great mercy, I worship Gaṅgā, whose drops of water destroy the multitude of sins. śrī-vedavyāsa-deva-vandanā Whose father is Parāśara and who is the father of Śukadeva. I worship that Vyāsa, who resides in Badarī, Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana. śrī-śukadeva-vandanā Whom, leaving home without undergoing ceremonies, having no duties, Dvaipāyana called out "O Son!", agitated by separation, and absorbed in him, the trees responded. I bow to that Muni who is the heart of all beings. Who spoke this essence of all scriptures, the unique lamp of knowledge for those desiring to cross the blinding darkness. Who spoke the confidential Purāṇa out of compassion for materialistic people. I surrender to that son of Vyāsa, the guru of munis. śrīmad-bhāgavata-vandanā That primeval Lord, the abode of mercy, the color of a Tamāla tree, the most beneficial incarnation. The bridge over the boundless ocean of material existence. We worship the Śrīmad Bhāgavatam. śrī-śrī-nāma-vandanā Glories, glories to the blissful form of the name of Murāri, which causes one to give up efforts in prescribed duties, meditation, and pūjā. Which, if taken even once somehow or other, bestows liberation to living entities. My supreme nectar, my only life and ornament. Sweeter than sweet, the most auspicious of auspicious things, the excellent fruit of the creeper of all Vedas, conscious by nature. Chanted even once, with faith or with negligence, O best of the Bhṛgus! The name of Kṛṣṇa delivers any human being. śrī-śrī-jagannātha-deva-vandanā On the shore of the great ocean, on the blue summit shining like gold. Residing inside the palace with his brother Balabhadra. Between them is Subhadrā; giving opportunity for service to all suras. May that Jagannātha Svāmī be visible to my eyes. To the resident of Nīlācala, the eternal Paramātmā. To Balabhadra and Subhadrā, and to you Jagannātha, obeisances. śrī-śrī-nṛsiṁha-deva-vandanā Obeisances to Narasiṁha, who gives joy to Prahlāda. Whose nails are like chisels on the stone-like chest of Hiraṇyakaśipu. Narasiṁha is here, Narasiṁha is there. Wherever I go, there is Narasiṁha. Narasiṁha is outside, Narasiṁha is in the heart. I take shelter of Narasiṁha, the origin. Śrī Narasiṁha, glories to Narasiṁha, glories glories to Narasiṁha. Lord of Prahlāda, glory to the bee of the lotus face of Padmā. On whose face is Vāgīśā, on whose chest is Lakṣmī. In whose heart is knowledge, I worship that Narasiṁha. The joy of Prahlāda's heart, the destroyer of the devotees' ignorance. Resplendent like the autumn moon, I bow to the lion-faced Hari. śrī-śrī-rāmacandra-vandanā Whose limbs are soft and dark like a blue lotus, with Sītā seated on his left side. Holding a great arrow and beautiful bow in his hands, I bow to Rāma, the lord of the Raghu dynasty. On the right is Lakṣmaṇa holding a bow, on the left is the auspicious Jānakī. In front is Mārutī, I bow to that best of the Raghus. śrī-śrī-daśāvatāra-vandanā You deliver the Vedas, support the worlds, lift the earth, tear apart the demon, deceive Bali, destroy the warrior class, conquer Paulastya, hold the plow, extend mercy, and bewilder the mlecchas. I offer obeisances to You, Kṛṣṇa, who assume these ten forms.
